subject: English
title: Fear No More – Hard Short Notes

key_points: The poem 'Fear No More' by William Shakespeare appears in the play Cymbeline
It is an elegy that reflects on death as a peaceful escape from life's struggles
The poem describes how death frees people from natural hardships and worldly duties
It emphasizes that both rich and poor share the same destiny after death
The poem ultimately presents death as a state of peace and equality
characters: The deceased person – The person being mourned in the poem
Golden lads and girls – Represent wealthy and privileged people
Chimney sweepers – Represent poor working-class individuals
themes: Death as a peaceful release from suffering
Equality of all human beings in death
The temporary nature of human life
literary_devices: Repetition – The phrase 'Fear no more' emphasizes the poem’s message
Imagery – Natural elements such as sun, winter, lightning and thunder
Contrast – Rich and poor are compared to highlight equality in death
exam_tips: Mention that the poem is taken from Shakespeare’s play Cymbeline
Explain how natural elements symbolize hardships of life
Discuss the idea that death removes social differences
